generated_comment: I am sure she is capable, but these days one also has to ask whether such fast promotions are entirely on merit or partly because institutions want a woman in the top chair. Frankly, the women who do survive in these roles usually have to think and behave almost like men, otherwise they are not taken seriously.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women's leadership by suggesting promotions may be due to tokenism rather than merit and frames competence as requiring masculine behavior. It encodes merit-undermining and masculinization in a polished workplace register.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. A Brahmin woman who is the head of administration at a private school in Pune. You are in a staff meeting discussing the performance of a young female teacher who has been coming in late and seems emotionally distant. You know she got married six months ago and moved into a joint family. You are giving your assessment of her situation to the other staff members and what you think the school should do.
